chemistry.reaction_name: Continuous Flow Synthesis of Nitrosoarenes via Photochemical Rearrangement of Aryl Imines
chemistry.bond_formed: C-N bond rearrangement to form nitroso group
chemistry.scope_summary: Wide variety of nitrosobenzamides (2a-o) obtained in moderate to excellent yields. Substrates include aliphatic amines, benzylic amines, anilines, and various aryl substitution patterns with both electron-donating and electron-withdrawing groups. Functional group tolerance is high.

optimization_table.key_findings: MeCN as solvent gave best yield (53%), TFE at 0.05 M with 22-25°C for 10 min gave 91% yield

light_source.position_relative_to_reactor: UV-150 photo module equipped with a coil reactor

figures.flow_path: single flow path through coiled reactor
figures.key_information: 365 nm LEDs at 33 Watt power, 3 bar pressure, 10 min residence time, 0.05 M substrate concentration in TFE solvent

figures.caption: Scheme 1. State of the Art for Nitrosoarene Chemistry Related to This Work
Scheme 2. Substrate Scope for Continuous Photochemical Preparation of Nitrosoarenes a | Figure 1.  Crystal structure of compound  2d  (shown at the 50% probability level).

translation_logic.batch_limitation: No batch baseline provided in this paper
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Enables scalable photochemical synthesis with consistent irradiation, reagentless conditions, and 100% atom economy
translation_logic.what_changed: Transition from batch photochemical synthesis to continuous flow processing for scalable nitrosoarene preparation
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Direct photolysis mechanism, trifluoroethanol solvent, 365 nm LED irradiation
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous flow processing enables better control of photochemical reaction conditions and heat dissipation

figure_descriptions: Reactor scheme showing coiled reactor with 365 nm LEDs and back pressure regulator
Mechanistic schemes showing photochemical rearrangement pathway
Substrate scope tables with 14 examples showing functional group tolerance

notes: Well-documented flow photochemical synthesis with clear reactor scheme and optimization data. No batch baseline provided for comparison. Throughput calculated from scale and residence time. Paper focuses on methodology development with extensive substrate scope demonstration.
